raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$310.00} by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. This company misrepresented its services by claiming this charge was a nonrefundable state filing fee when, in fact, my order with them was canceled and no filing was ever made with the Louisiana XXXX of XXXX. I later confirmed this in my account portal, which shows Canceled and Not Incorporated. 

On XX/XX/year>, I disputed this charge with Citi. Citi opened Case # XXXX for the {$310.00} transaction and Case # XXXX for a separate {$190.00} charge by the same merchant on XX/XX/year>. Citi correctly issued me a permanent credit for the {$190.00} charge, recognizing the merchants misrepresentation. However, Citi reversed my conditional credit for the {$310.00} charge and denied the dispute. 

Citis denial letter, dated XX/XX/year>, states that they requested information from me on XX/XX/year> with a response deadline of XX/XX/year>. This explanation is factually impossible. The disputed transaction did not occur until XX/XX/year>, and my dispute was not filed until XX/XX/year>. It is not possible for Citi to have requested information months before the charge even existed. This indicates that Citi closed my case improperly and without a fair investigation. 

In total, I received {$270.00} in partial refunds from the merchant and {$190.00} from Citis permanent credit. The remaining {$310.00} has not been refunded, despite the fact that the merchant never performed the service or remitted any payment to the state. 

I have been a Citi customer in good standing for years, always paying my balance in full and never making late payments. I expect Citi to handle disputes fairly under the Fair Credit Billing Act. In this case, Citi failed to investigate properly, relied on impossible dates, and left me responsible for a fraudulent charge. 

I am requesting that Citi reinstate the {$310.00} credit immediately.
